_source: Canonical manifest of the SQL table functions this extension registers. Gated against the register_table() calls in src/*.rs by tests/functions_manifest.rs, so it cannot drift from the actual surface. Consumers pin a copy of this file (e.g. the docs repo) rather than hand-listing the functions.

functions.name: read_ags
functions.returns: one group as a typed table — _id, _parent_id, then one column per heading (typed from the file's TYPE row). Reads local / http(s):// / s3://.

functions.name: read_ags_text
functions.returns: the same typed table, from an inline AGS4 string (no filesystem).

functions.name: ags_groups
functions.returns: (group, n_rows, n_headings, parent) — the file's group list.

functions.name: ags_headings
functions.returns: (group, heading, unit, ags_type, sql_type, status, is_key, ordinal) — the per-heading schema, enriched with the dictionary's KEY status.

functions.name: ags_dictionary
functions.returns: the embedded standard AGS dictionary as a table (group, heading, status, ags_type, unit, description, …).

functions.name: ags_relationships
functions.returns: (child, parent, shared_keys) — the spec parent→child graph that _parent_id follows.

functions.name: ags_rules
functions.returns: the AGS4 numbered-rule catalogue (rule, title, severity, fixable) — the extension lists the rules; the CLI/library run them.

functions.name: load_ags
functions.returns: (seq, stmt) — CREATE-TABLE DDL to materialise every group into an indexed, repeat-/remote-queryable store.
